essence: Banking/government changes again blocked the usual transfer to Mary, so Daniel agreed to make the payment once David sends bank details (due by tomorrow), while David's recently-bought laptop screen suddenly failed — possibly sun exposure — and Jan is investigating 32-inch monitors so both staff get computer+monitor+keyboard.

actions.text: Send Mary's bank details to Daniel so he can make the payment by tomorrow
Jan to investigate 32-inch monitors for both staff machines

essence: Post-meetup social: Joy's smoothie shop opens Monday amid surprise Thai signage-tax and music-licensing fine rules; David walked through his POEM YAML/Handlebars/JSON-Schema workflow transpiler that compiles conversational workflows into a deterministic n8n/ComfyUI-style node graph with human-in-the-loop UI; and he debated abandoning his 12-year ideasmen Google Workspace for fresh per-agent free Gmail identities plus Cloudflare-hosted domains.

actions.text: Send JSON workflow structures to meetup friend so he can propose prompts/image concepts to visualize and animate agent architectures

essence: David recounted one-shot-generating a full app spec, a Python UI reconstruction, and an entire Mermaid diagram suite (flowcharts, entity graphs, subsystem maps, swimlanes) from a single JSON spec — with HTML views regenerated straight from updated JSON — and learned the friend's cheap pattern of crafting prompts in Gemini then executing them via kie.ai's playground (~6 cents per infographic).

Cafe deep-dive pairing David's two-hour agent run that refined a global-plus-per-brain semantic tagging ontology across 37 brains / ~640 files with a friend's memoir LifeGraph concept — word-level force-aligned transcripts, entity-spelling correction propagated via outbox workers, and Postgres+PGVector replacing Pinecone so SQL and embeddings live under one hood.

Post-meetup session where David explained his Ralphie multi-wave batch loop (planned+executed a 600-file brain tagging job during a 2-hour meeting, self-healing its own skills) and crystallised the AppyStack recipe paradigm — ship a tested React/Vite/Tailwind/Socket.IO foundation plus prompt recipes so agents rebuild whole CRUD apps from JSON-modeled entities, a new 'improve the prompt, not the app' distribution model.

Saturday meetup debate sparked by Jack Dorsey's Block layoffs on whether AI-first solo operators now beat teams; David argued agent-to-agent workflows restore team amplification — discovery documents over project management, Jan trained as his client-meeting proxy seeded by Loom transcripts, all human messages passing through AI — against the counterview that live meetings create emergent meaning and accountability stays human.

Meetup discussion on capturing the 'why' behind organizational decisions that systems of record miss — context graphs, intent engineering as the step after context engineering, introspective journaling as organizational memory, with Klarna's wrong-metric AI customer-service failure as the cautionary case.

Pre-meetup exploration of agent-to-agent communication: unified monorepo personal agents versus separate per-persona systems (wife vs boss), agents compensating for human weaknesses like an introvert running an extroverted online persona, and measuring empathy/ambiguity-tolerance/state-awareness as AI capacities.

At the Saturday AI engineers meetup David dictated his second-brain roadmap — OMI device ingestion endpoint, frontmatter and tagging-ontology audit across 37 brains / 600 files with upstream sources, a psychological travel-preference profile (post-noon departures, pre-5pm arrivals) feeding DTV border-run planning — and proposed multi-OMI transcript capture piped to NotebookLM podcasts so the meetup can go hybrid without losing the in-person core.

actions.text: Research how to ingest OMI device transcripts into own endpoint/app and design the receiving endpoint
Audit all brains for frontmatter coverage and per-brain tagging ontology, then run a multi-hour three-brains-at-a-time processing flow
Experiment sending meetup transcripts to NotebookLM to auto-generate a podcast for remote participants

essence: Agents in the Wild lightning talks: Yohan's OpenClaw pattern of exhaustive main-agent planning with memoryless Docker sub-agents killed after each task, and Ian open-sourcing his personal agent Samantha/KyberBot that morning — Claude Code harness, Kybernesis-style sleep-time memory engine, soul/user markdown files, no infra cost beyond the Max subscription.

loop.label: agents in the wild lightning talks

essence: Meetup host demoed Ghost, his Claude Code + Chrome MCP personal agent, auto-publishing an event across Sola, Luma, Meetup and Facebook via browser automation (avoiding paid APIs) with CRUD-organized per-platform skill files, arguing for token-heavy over deterministic design because token costs trend toward zero.

loop.label: agents in the wild lightning talks

essence: At the Friday meetup David demoed his Ralphie wave-based loop live-rebuilding an application from combined JSON/YAML/Mermaid specs on the Stacked (AppyStack) harness — React+Express+Socket.IO with JSON documents instead of a database — tracing its lineage from Jeff Huntley's Ralph Wiggum loop through the playbook variant to his own task-agent/pre-flight-validation version.

Amid a fridge-fitting errand David directed his agent to check both the second brain and the plugin for Ralphie and report its actual capabilities, frustrated after a 'disastrous' conversation in another window trying to figure this out.

actions.text: Check the Ralphie second brain and plugin and report what capabilities Ralphie has
